summary: QA acceptance review passed. The SERP Analysis Agent implementation provides typed Zod boundaries, a stable analyzeSerpResults() public function, docs-aligned Agent 2 prompt construction, injected safeJsonCompletion use only through LlmClient, deterministic local heuristic fallback, required output normalization, focused tests, README documentation, and no observed scope creep into orchestration, persistence, API, UI, provider, auth, cron, deployment, or paid-service setup.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Reusable SERP Analysis Agent under agents/ with typed input/output/signal Zod schemas and stable public analyze function.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: agents/serp-analysis-agent.ts defines SerpAnalysisInputSchema, SerpAnalysisOutputSchema, SerpWeakSignalSchema, SerpStrongSignalSchema, related inferred types, and analyzeSerpResults(); agents/index.ts re-exports the public boundary.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Prompt aligned with docs/08 Agent 2 and calls existing safeJsonCompletion through injected LlmClient only.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: buildSerpAnalysisMessages() mirrors docs/08 Agent 2 keyword/country/language/SERP JSON, weak and strong signal instructions, and JSON schema; safeJsonCompletion is only reached when options.llmClient is provided.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Deterministic heuristic fallback analyzes mock SERP-like results without keys, env reads, network calls, DB writes, or paid services.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: generateHeuristicSerpAnalysis() and helper functions operate on provided inputs only. Source scan found no process.env, fetch, Prisma, DB, provider configuration, or paid-service wiring in the reviewed agent/test files.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Output includes serpWeaknessSummary, weakSignals, strongSignals, and clamped 0-100 serpWeaknessScoreHint.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: SerpAnalysisOutputSchema enforces the required fields and normalizes serpWeaknessScoreHint through normalizeSerpWeaknessScoreHint().

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Fallback recognizes generic articles, forum/community, PDF/government/old/poor-UX, missing interactive tools, mature SaaS/tool competition, and empty SERP deterministically.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: Fallback signal collection covers the required weak and strong signal categories, and tests assert the main weak SERP, strong competition, and empty-result paths.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: Tests cover LLM success, malformed/rejected LLM fallback, score clamping/normalization, generic article/no-tool weak signals on mock SERP, strong competition signals, and empty SERP behavior.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: tests/serp-analysis-agent.test.ts contains six focused cases covering the requested scenarios.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: README documents slice, heuristic fallback, no-secret/no-network policy, and test command.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: README.md includes a SERP Analysis Agent section documenting the slice, fallback behavior, no-secret/no-network/no-DB/no-paid-service policy, npm test, and npm run build.

acceptance_criteria_review.criterion: No opportunity analysis, scoring, scan orchestration, persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, real LLM wiring, SERP provider changes, or paid setup.
acceptance_criteria_review.evidence: Reviewed source changes are limited to the agent boundary, index exports, focused tests, and README documentation; no forbidden product surface was added.

tests_run.result: Validated 7 active task files, including M3-SERP-ANALYSIS-AGENT-001.
Passed: 22 tests across 6 suites, including 6 SERP Analysis Agent tests.
Passed: Next.js production build compiled, type-checked, generated static pages, and exited successfully. Existing non-fatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings were observed.
Passed with Git LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings only; no whitespace errors reported.

decisions: Accepted the SERP Analysis Agent public contract and exports as stable for this M3 slice.
Accepted the prompt alignment with docs/08 Agent 2 and the injected LlmClient-only safeJsonCompletion boundary.
Accepted the deterministic heuristic fallback and output clamping/normalization behavior.
Accepted the focused test coverage and README documentation as satisfying the task acceptance criteria.
Treated existing project coordination JSON changes and incoming handoff files as workflow state; no product files were edited during QA.
risks: The heuristic weights are intentionally lightweight and should remain an input hint, not the final opportunity score, in later milestones.
The build still reports existing non-fatal Windows SWC native package warnings despite successful exit.
Fallback tuning may need adjustment once real scan orchestration provides broader SERP examples.
requested_action: Integrate or close M3-SERP-ANALYSIS-AGENT-001 according to the project workflow.
